title: marsdenia engleriana
intro: Marsdenia engleriana, also known as the Milk Vine, is a plant species native to the Philippines, characterized by its long, twining stems and clusters of white, fragrant flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Marsdenia engleriana include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of decoctions, infusions, or poultices, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and safety.
